title: Within-race-lap query ranking sidecar
summary: The decision is relative: on a given lap, only a few drivers are realistic pit candidates.
rationale: The decision is relative: on a given lap, only a few drivers are realistic pit candidates. CatBoost exposes pairwise and groupwise ranking objectives, which can train directly on within-query ordering rather than only global classification; that often aligns well with ROC-AUC tasks where the main challenge is ranking hard positives above contemporaneous negatives.
implementation_hint: Define each query as (Year, Race, LapNumber). Train a CatBoost ranker such as PairLogit or YetiRankPairwise to rank drivers by next-lap pit likelihood within the query, using the current engineered features. Convert OOF rank scores to probabilities with fold-wise sigmoid calibration only if needed for blending.
expected_effect: Should sharpen local discrimination on crowded decision windows and add ensemble diversity relative to pure pointwise GBDTs.
risk: Ranking loss may underweight absolute calibration and can be noisy on laps with zero or one positive; keep it as a sidecar, not a full replacement.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
